Priority escalation summary

Short escalation summary.

Priority escalation guide

Priority escalation guides matter when they explain which workload classes can request higher scheduling precedence during constrained capacity windows.

The guide should define approval checkpoints, incident triggers, and rollback conditions so operators know when temporary escalation is justified.

It should also clarify how escalation interacts with queue fairness, dashboard evidence, and fallback queue controls after the elevated window ends.

Related limit guides
